method: gh run list --limit 200 with createdAt window filter
Computed temporal overlap: which runs had createdAt <= 21:35:45Z <= updatedAt
gh run view <id> --log for candidate runs
Match strength: STRONG (order_id OR exact $485.02 within 80 chars of AMD OR explicit script invocation) vs WEAK (AMD + sell-related token same line)

weak_matches.name: Price Monitor — Momentum Breakout
weak_matches.match_reason: AMD appears in LONG TICKERS scan list — informational only, NOT an order. Monitor only generates BUY signals.

weak_matches.name: Emergency Close — autonomous position closer
weak_matches.match_reason: Matched script was scripts/emergency_close_20260603.py (already processed and skipped), NOT AMD. Run started 11min AFTER the order.

temporal_window_evidence.interpretation: AMD order arrived in a 4m16s gap between scheduled cron waves; ZERO workflows were active at submission.

exit_monitor_21_48_05_iso_utc.interpretation: AMD already absent from positions list at 21:48Z, consistent with close happening externally.

rationale_for_next_action: The submitter_source field 'access_key' applies to ANY REST call using the Alpaca API key (MCP, Cloudflare Worker, manual curl). The client_order_id prefix is the only deterministic discriminator we have without GitHub Actions runner logs (none of which match).
